title: dryopteris hawaiiensis
intro: Dryopteris hawaiiensis, also known as Hawaiian wood fern, is a species of fern native to Hawaii, characterized by its distinctive fronds and woody rhizomes. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris hawaiiensis are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Dryopteris hawaiiensis is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical use, as well as being used in tinctures and capsules for its medicinal benefits.
